memes.label: Arms

memes.label: Battle

memes.label: Camelot
memes.description: 'The principal seat of King Arthur''s court — the geographical and symbolic centre of the Arthurian world where the Round Table assembles, quests begin and end, and the great events of the Morte unfold.'

memes.label: Castle
memes.description: 'The castle as military fortification, seat of lordship, and narrative device in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Challenge
memes.description: 'The formal challenge as chivalric ritual, narrative device, and test of honour in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Champion
memes.description: 'The champion as a knight who fights on behalf of another in trial by combat or judicial duel in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Chapel
memes.description: 'A sacred architectural space in the Arthurian world, serving as a site of knightly vigils, adventure encounters, defensive stands, and religious observance in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Chivalry
memes.description: 'The code of knightly conduct central to Thomas Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," encompassing prowess, courtesy, loyalty, and service to God and king.'

memes.label: Christening
memes.description: 'Christening and baptism as markers of Christian identity, narrative thresholds, and spiritual transformation in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Cornwall
memes.description: 'A kingdom in southwestern Britain, ruled by King Mark, central to the narrative of Sir Tristram and the truage conflict with Ireland.'

memes.label: Counsel
memes.description: 'Counsel in Malory denotes the giving and taking of advice as the foundation of good kingship, political deliberation, and knightly decision-making in "Le Morte d''Arthur."'

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: 'A cardinal knightly virtue in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," manifested in battle, quest, and moral trials — valued both as martial bravery and as the moral fortitude to act rightly under pressure.'

memes.label: Courtesy
memes.description: 'Courtesy as a foundational chivalric virtue, the code of refined conduct that distinguishes the true knight in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Crown
memes.description: 'The crown as symbol of kingship, sovereign authority, and rightful rule in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Custom
memes.description: 'The binding force of tradition and established practice in "Le Morte d''Arthur," governing everything from royal feasts to castle passages; customs may be honorable, arbitrary, or malign, and a knight''s response to custom reveals his character.'

memes.label: Dame Bragwaine
memes.description: 'The loyal maiden and confidante of La Beale Isoud in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur"; keeper of the love potion, messenger between Tristram and Isoud, and steadfast guardian of their secret love.'

memes.label: Dame Lionesse
memes.description: 'Sister of Sir Gringamore, beloved of Sir Gareth, whom she tested and ultimately married after his victory over the Red Knight of the Red Launds'

memes.label: Dame Nyneve
memes.description: 'One of the damosels of the lake who learned enchantment from Merlin, imprisoned him under a stone, and became a protector of King Arthur.'

memes.label: Damosel
memes.description: 'A young noblewoman in Arthurian romance who serves as messenger, quest-giver, or companion to knights-errant.'

memes.label: Dragon
memes.description: 'The dragon as symbolic creature, heraldic emblem, and dream-vision figure in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: 'Prophetic and symbolic dreams as vehicles of divine warning and narrative foreshadowing in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Duke
memes.description: 'The duke as high noble rank and territorial lord in the feudal hierarchy of Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Duke of Cornwall
memes.description: 'The Duke of Cornwall, first husband of Igraine and the lord whose war against Uther Pendragon set in motion the events leading to Arthur''s conception'

memes.label: Dwarf
memes.description: 'Dwarfs as messengers, servants, and supernatural figures in the chivalric romance world of Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Emperor Lucius
memes.description: 'The Roman Emperor who demanded tribute from King Arthur and died in the great battle against the Britons'

memes.label: Enchantment
memes.description: 'Supernatural manipulation through magical arts in "Le Morte d''Arthur," wielded by figures such as Merlin and Morgan le Fay to alter perception, control action, and shape destiny.'

memes.label: Envy
memes.description: 'Envy as a destructive force driving discord, rivalry, and tragedy among knights in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Ettard
memes.description: 'A proud lady of Arthurian legend who scorned the love of Sir Pelleas and was deceived by Sir Gawaine, leading to her death of sorrow'

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: 'A multi-layered concept in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" spanning religious belief, feudal loyalty, and personal trustworthiness — invoked in oaths, prayers, and judgments of character throughout the narrative.'

memes.label: Fealty
memes.description: 'Fealty as the feudal bond of sworn loyalty between lord and vassal in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur, and its tension with other obligations.'

memes.label: Feast
memes.description: 'The feast as political assembly, narrative framing device, and ritual occasion in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Feast of Pentecost
memes.description: 'The supreme feast-day of the Arthurian calendar — the annual gathering at which the knights of the Round Table renew their oaths, quests are announced and concluded, and the great spiritual events of the Morte, including the coming of the Sangreal, unfold.'

memes.label: Fellowship
memes.description: 'The bond of mutual loyalty and shared purpose among knights in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," manifested in both the Round Table and in smaller bands of warriors who fight and adventure together.'

memes.label: Forest
memes.description: 'The forest as setting of adventure, domain of the unknown, and space of transformation in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Gaheris
memes.description: 'Youngest son of King Lot and Morgawse, brother to Gawaine, Agravaine, and Gareth; a knight of the Round Table who rode alongside Gawaine and was freed from captivity by Sir Launcelot.'

memes.label: Gentleness
memes.description: 'Gentleness as the noble quality of character—combining birth, bearing, and moral excellence—that defines the ideal knight in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Giant
memes.description: 'Giants as monstrous antagonists and ultimate tests of knightly prowess in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Gouvernail
memes.description: 'The loyal servant and governor of Sir Tristram, who accompanied him from youth through his greatest adventures.'

memes.label: Grace
memes.description: 'Grace in Malory denotes divine favour and the unmerited assistance of God that enables knightly success, ordains kingship, and distinguishes the spiritually worthy in "Le Morte d''Arthur."'

memes.label: Griffin
memes.description: 'The griffin in Malory as a heraldic charge and a dream-beast of destruction, marking the boundary between the chivalric and the monstrous.'

memes.label: Hardiness
memes.description: 'Hardiness as the knightly virtue of physical endurance, courage under hardship, and relentless persistence in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Helm
memes.description: 'The helm as protective headpiece, target of combat strikes, and marker of knightly identity in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Heraldry
memes.description: 'The system of arms, shields, banners, and visual identification that structures chivalric recognition and military organisation in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Hermit
memes.description: 'A religious solitary in "Le Morte d''Arthur" who lives apart from the world and serves as spiritual guide, confessor, interpreter of dreams, and healer to the knights who encounter him in the wilderness.'

memes.label: Homage
memes.description: 'The formal ceremony of feudal submission and acknowledgment of lordship in "Le Morte d''Arthur," binding vassal to lord through a public act of kneeling and oath-swearing that establishes political obligation.'

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: 'The public recognition of knightly worth in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — both an internal moral quality and an external reputation earned through deeds and lost through disgrace.'

memes.label: Horse
memes.description: 'The horse as essential equipment of the knight, instrument of mobility, and marker of status in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Igraine
memes.description: 'Duchess of Cornwall and later Queen of England; mother of King Arthur by Uther Pendragon, and of Morgawse, Elaine, and Morgan le Fay by the Duke of Cornwall.'

memes.label: Joust
memes.description: 'The joust as the premier form of knightly single combat in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur, its customs, and its narrative role.'

memes.label: Judgment
memes.description: 'Judgment as legal decision, trial by combat, and divine verdict in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: King
memes.description: 'Kingship as institution, the office of the king, and royal authority in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: King Agwisance
memes.description: 'King of Ireland who led five thousand men against King Arthur and was defeated in the great battles of the early reign.'

memes.label: King Anguish
memes.description: 'King of Ireland, father of La Beale Isoud, and central figure in Sir Tristram''s early adventures'

memes.label: King Arthur
memes.description: 'The central sovereign of Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," the noble king and conqueror who establishes the Round Table fellowship and whose reign ends in tragedy through the betrayals of those closest to him.'

memes.label: King Ban
memes.description: 'King of Benwick in France, father of Sir Launcelot, and one of King Arthur''s earliest and most stalwart allies'

memes.label: King Bors
memes.description: 'King Bors of Gaul, brother of King Ban and a steadfast ally of King Arthur in the early wars against the rebellious kings of Britain.'

memes.label: King Brandegoris
memes.description: 'King Brandegoris of Stranggore, one of the eleven rebel kings who opposed King Arthur in the early wars of his reign.'

memes.label: King Carados
memes.description: 'A mighty king and knight who appears both as a rebel against King Arthur and as a formidable opponent in tournaments and quests'
aliases: Sir Carados of the Dolorous Tower

memes.label: King Clariance
memes.description: 'King Clariance of Northumberland, one of the rebel kings who opposed King Arthur in the early wars of consolidation'

memes.label: King Claudas
memes.description: 'King of the Waste Lands and the great enemy of Kings Ban and Bors, whose wars of conquest drove the infant Lancelot and his cousins into exile'
aliases: King Claudas of the Waste Lands

memes.label: King Cradelment of North Wales
memes.description: 'A rebel king of North Wales who fought against King Arthur in the early wars of his reign.'

memes.label: King Idres
memes.description: 'A rebel king who opposed King Arthur in the early wars, fought alongside King Lot and King Nentres, and was eventually subdued and placed in the City of Nauntes'

memes.label: King Leodegrance
memes.description: 'King of Cameliard, father of Queen Guenever, and an early ally of King Arthur who gifted the Round Table to the young king as a wedding present'

memes.label: King Lot
memes.description: 'King of Lothian and Orkney, one of the rebel kings against Arthur who later swore fealty to the Round Table'

memes.label: King Mark
memes.description: 'The king of Cornwall in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," uncle to Sir Tristram — a figure whose jealousy and suspicion toward Tristram drive one of the central narrative threads of the cycle.'

memes.label: King Meliodas
memes.description: 'King of Liones and father of Sir Tristram, a noble ruler who endured imprisonment and familial strife.'

memes.label: King Nentres
memes.description: 'King of Garlot, husband of Arthur''s sister Elaine, one of the rebel kings who opposed Arthur in the early wars before submitting to his rule'

memes.label: King of Northgalis
memes.description: 'A king who frequently appeared in tournaments throughout the chronicle, often as an adversary to Sir Tristram and his allies.'

memes.label: King Pellam
memes.description: 'King of Listeneise, the Maimed King struck by the Dolorous Stroke, later healed by Galahad in the quest of the Sangreal.'

memes.label: King Rience
memes.description: 'King of North Wales and Ireland who defeated eleven kings and demanded King Arthur''s beard to trim his mantle, the most audacious symbolic challenge of Arthur''s early reign.'

memes.label: King Uriens
memes.description: 'King of the land of Gore, husband of Morgan le Fay, and one of the early rebel kings who later became Arthur''s ally'

memes.label: Kinsman
memes.description: 'The kinsman in Malory as the strongest bond of obligation in the chivalric world — and the most devastating when broken.'

memes.label: Knight-Errant
memes.description: 'The knight-errant as the fundamental unit of chivalric adventure in Malory — a wandering knight who rides in search of arms, honour, and marvels.'

memes.label: Knighthood
memes.description: 'The institution, ideals, and solemn obligations of the knightly order in "Le Morte d''Arthur," conferred through a ceremonial dubbing and binding the recipient to a sacred code of honor, mercy, and service.'

memes.label: La Beale Isoud
memes.description: 'The Irish princess beloved of Sir Tristram, the fair Isoud whose love story with Tristram is one of the central romances of the Arthurian cycle.'

memes.label: Lady
memes.description: 'The lady as noblewoman, object of chivalric devotion, political agent, and narrative catalyst in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Lady of the Lake
memes.description: 'A supernatural enchantress in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — the giver of Excalibur, the foster-mother of Sir Launcelot, and the eventual entrapper of Merlin, embodying feminine magic as both nurturer and destroyer.'

memes.label: Lineage
memes.description: 'Lineage as the determinant of noble identity, the basis of political legitimacy, and a recurrent source of narrative revelation in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Lordship
memes.description: 'The feudal bond of lordship — the reciprocal obligation between lord and vassal that structures the political world of Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Love
memes.description: 'A central force in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — celebrated as a wellspring of knightly prowess and critiqued as the passion that brings down Arthur''s kingdom, never resolved into simple moral judgement.'

memes.label: Maiden
memes.description: 'The maiden in Malory as a social and spiritual category — the unmarried woman whose state of maidenhood carries both worldly and sacred significance.'

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: 'Marriage as a political, dynastic, and narrative institution in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur, binding houses and shaping the fates of knights and kingdoms.'

memes.label: Marvel
memes.description: 'The experience of the supernatural in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — the encounter with wonders that exceed natural explanation, from miraculous swords to prophetic dreams, marking the intrusion of divine or magical power into the chivalric world.'

memes.label: Mass
memes.description: 'The Mass as central religious ritual, temporal marker, and narrative setting in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Mercy
memes.description: 'A recurring moral demand in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — the plea of the defeated for life and the expectation that true knights temper victory with compassion, though mercy is often denied with tragic consequences.'

memes.label: Merlin
memes.description: 'The enchanter and prophet of Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," whose supernatural knowledge guides Arthur''s rise to power and whose prophecies establish the tragic horizon within which the entire narrative unfolds.'

memes.label: Miracle
memes.description: 'Divine miraculous intervention as a sign of election, providence, and supernatural sanction in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Mordred
memes.description: 'Arthur''s illegitimate son in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," whose ambition and treachery bring about the final battle at Salisbury and the destruction of the Round Table — the instrument of the tragedy Merlin foretold.'

memes.label: Morgan le Fay
memes.description: 'Arthur''s half-sister in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — a figure of ambiguous and often hostile magic whose enchantments repeatedly test the knights of the Round Table and complicate the boundary between ally and adversary.'

memes.label: Queen Morgawse
memes.description: 'Queen of Orkney, half-sister of King Arthur and mother of Sir Gawaine, Sir Gareth, Sir Gaheris, Sir Agravaine, and Mordred; her lineage ties the Orkney clan to the throne of Camelot.'

memes.label: Oath
memes.description: 'The formal mechanism of binding promise in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — the spoken pledge by which knights bind themselves to kings, quests, and each other, and whose violation constitutes the gravest moral failure.'

memes.label: Pedivere
memes.description: 'A knight who killed his wife in a jealous rage; condemned by Sir Launcelot to bear her head to Queen Guenever, he later became a holy hermit.'

memes.label: Penance
memes.description: 'The religious practice of penance — confession, contrition, and satisfaction — as narrative mechanism for moral restoration in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: "Preface of William Caxton"
memes.description: "Preface to the 1485 edition"

memes.label: Prison
memes.description: 'Imprisonment and captivity as narrative device, chivalric test, and condition of the warrior in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Prophecy
memes.description: 'Supernatural foreknowledge in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," primarily delivered through Merlin, that shapes the destiny of kings and kingdoms and drives the narrative toward its tragic conclusion.'

memes.label: Prowess
memes.description: 'The knightly ideal of martial excellence in "Le Morte d''Arthur," encompassing skill in arms, physical strength, and the demonstrated capacity to prevail in single combat or battle.'

memes.label: Queen Guenever
memes.description: 'Arthur''s queen in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," whose adulterous love for Sir Launcelot becomes both the inspiration for his greatest deeds and the catalyst for the destruction of the Round Table fellowship.'

memes.label: Quest
memes.description: 'A central narrative structure in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," the knightly journey undertaken in pursuit of a specific objective — whether a physical object, a person, or spiritual enlightenment.'

memes.label: Questing Beast
memes.description: 'The Questing Beast as supernatural portent and narrative catalyst in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Realm
memes.description: 'The political and territorial domain of a sovereign in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur, encompassing the land, its people, and the obligations of rule that bind king to subject.'

memes.label: Religious Houses
memes.description: 'The network of abbeys, monasteries, nunneries, and hermitages that form the spiritual infrastructure of the Arthurian world — places of healing, sanctuary, burial, and moral counsel for knights and kings alike.'

memes.label: Repentance
memes.description: 'Repentance as moral reckoning, narrative turning-point, and spiritual prerequisite in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Revenge
memes.description: 'The pursuit of violent retaliation for injury or death in "Le Morte d''Arthur," a primary driver of narrative conflict that operates in persistent tension with the chivalric ideals of mercy and forgiveness.'

memes.label: Round Table
memes.description: 'The central institution of King Arthur''s court, a fellowship of knights bound by oaths of loyalty and chivalry, symbolized by a circular table where all knights sit as equals.'

memes.label: Sangreal
memes.description: 'The sacred quest for the Holy Grail (Sangreal), the highest spiritual adventure of the Round Table, achieved only by Sir Galahad, Sir Percival, and Sir Bors.'

memes.label: Seneschal
memes.description: 'The office of seneschal as steward and chief officer of the royal household in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur, embodied principally in Sir Kay.'

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: 'The antithesis of knightly worship in "Le Morte d''Arthur," representing public dishonour, moral failure, and the loss of reputation that every knight fears above death itself.'

memes.label: Shield
memes.description: 'The shield as defensive arm, heraldic canvas, and instrument of identity in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Siege
memes.description: 'The military tactic of surrounding and assaulting a fortified place in "Le Morte d''Arthur," a recurrent narrative device that drives campaign-level conflict, tests knightly valor, and frames the geography of Arthurian warfare.'

memes.label: Sin
memes.description: 'Sin in Malory encompasses the transgression of divine and chivalric law, driving the narrative through its consequences and motivating the quest for redemption in "Le Morte d''Arthur."'

memes.label: Sir Accolon
memes.description: 'Accolon of Gaul, lover of Morgan le Fay, who wielded Excalibur against King Arthur in a duel orchestrated by Morgan; defeated when Arthur recovered the sword, and died confessing the treason.'

memes.label: Sir Agravaine
memes.description: 'Son of King Lot of Orkney and Margawse, brother of Sir Gawaine, Gaheris, and Gareth; a Knight of the Round Table.'

memes.label: Sir Andred
memes.description: 'Cousin of Sir Tristram and spy for King Mark of Cornwall; a treacherous knight who repeatedly betrayed Tristram to the king, ultimately meeting his death in the final conflicts of the Morte.'

memes.label: Sir Bagdemagus
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table and king who departed court to seek adventures and prove his worth'

memes.label: Sir Balan
memes.description: 'The younger brother of Sir Balin, slain by him in mutual combat when neither recognised the other'

memes.label: Sir Balin
memes.description: 'The tragic knight of Book II, known as Balin le Savage, who draws the fateful sword and unwittingly slays his own brother Balan.'

memes.label: Sir Baudwin of Britain
memes.description: 'A trusted knight of King Arthur, made constable of England and later appointed governor of the realm alongside Sir Constantine.'

memes.label: Sir Beaumains
memes.description: 'The alias of Sir Gareth during his first year at King Arthur''s court, meaning "Fair-hands," given mockingly by Sir Kay'

memes.label: Sir Bedivere
memes.description: 'Knight of the Round Table who fought alongside Sir Lionel in the Roman War and served as one of King Arthur''s trusted commanders'

memes.label: Sir Belliance
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table and brother of Sir Lamorak de Galis; known for his courteous combat and the deep bond of mutual recognition and care between the two brothers.'

memes.label: Sir Blamore
memes.description: 'Knight of the Round Table, brother of Sir Bleoberis de Ganis, and cousin of Sir Launcelot'

memes.label: Sir Bleoberis
memes.description: 'A Knight of the Round Table and cousin of Sir Launcelot, known for the adventure of Sir Segwarides''s lady and his role in Launcelot''s rescue'

memes.label: Sir Bors
memes.description: 'One of the three Grail knights in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," distinguished by steadfast loyalty and moral judgment — the knight who chooses to aid a maiden over saving his own brother, and is judged righteous for it.'

memes.label: Sir Brandiles
memes.description: 'A Knight of the Round Table who jousted with Sir Tristram and served alongside Sir Kay and Sir Uwaine'

memes.label: Sir Brastias
memes.description: 'A knight of the Duke of Cornwall who became one of King Arthur trusted companions and warden of the north.'

memes.label: Sir Breunor
memes.description: 'The lord of the Castle Pluere (the Weeping Castle), whose foul custom of beheading ladies was ended by Sir Tristram.'

memes.label: Sir Breuse Saunce Pité
memes.description: 'A villainous and cowardly knight of the Arthurian world, known for preying on the weak and fleeing from any knight who could match him in combat'

memes.label: Sir Brian de les Isles
memes.description: 'A noble knight of the Isles who fought against Sir Launcelot and later delivered prisoners from a besieged castle'

memes.label: Sir Cador of Cornwall
memes.description: 'A knight of Cornwall and Round Table companion, father of Sir Constantine who succeeded King Arthur.'

memes.label: Sir Carados of the Dolorous Tower
memes.description: 'A noble knight of Scotland and the dolorous tower, brother of Sir Turquine, slain by Sir Launcelot.'

memes.label: Sir Constantine
memes.description: 'Sir Constantine of Cornwall, son of Sir Cador, named heir to King Arthur and later king of Britain'

memes.label: Sir Dagonet
memes.description: 'King Arthur''s fool and mock-knight, a figure of courtly entertainment whose misadventures provide comic relief in the Arthurian chronicle'

memes.label: Sir Damas
memes.description: 'The elder, false brother of Sir Ontzlake; a treacherous knight who imprisoned his own brother and forced King Arthur to serve as his champion in a trial by combat.'

memes.label: Sir Darras
memes.description: 'An old knight and lord of a castle who imprisoned Sir Tristram, Sir Palomides, and Sir Dinadan, but later released them upon learning their identities.'

memes.label: Sir Dinadan
memes.description: 'A witty and pragmatic Knight of the Round Table, companion of Sir Tristram and the court jester among knights'

memes.label: Sir Dinant
memes.description: 'Sir Dinant, a knight who encountered a giant in the forest and later spoke with King Mark at a fair fountain concerning Sir Tristram.'

memes.label: Sir Dinas the Seneschal
memes.description: 'The loyal seneschal and trusted friend who served King Mark of Cornwall and aided Sir Tristram and Dame Bragwaine through times of danger'

memes.label: Sir Dodinas le Savage
memes.description: 'Sir Dodinas le Savage, a Knight of the Round Table who appeared in tournaments, battles, and in the company of Sir Sagramore le Desirous'

memes.label: Sir Driant
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table who jousted at tournaments and aided in the rescue of Sir Launcelot from Morgan le Fay.'

memes.label: Sir Ector
memes.description: 'The faithful knight who fostered the infant Arthur, father of Sir Kay, and witness to the drawing of the sword from the stone'

memes.label: Sir Edward of the Red Castle
memes.description: 'A knight of the Red Castle who fought alongside Sir Hue against Sir Uwaine in a tournament passage of arms'

memes.label: Sir Fergus
memes.description: 'A knight of Cornwall who served as a loyal companion to Sir Tristram and tended him during his madness.'

memes.label: Sir Florence
memes.description: 'Knight of the Round Table who commanded a contingent of one hundred knights in the Roman War under King Arthur'

memes.label: Sir Frol
memes.description: 'Sir Frol of the Out Isles, a knight slain by Sir Lamorak de Galis, whose brother Sir Belliance sought vengeance.'
aliases: Sir Frol of the Out Isles

memes.label: Sir Gahalantine
memes.description: 'Sir Gahalantine, a Knight of the Round Table who fought in tournaments alongside Sir Mador and Sir Mordred'

memes.label: Sir Galahad
memes.description: 'The pure knight of the Round Table who achieves the Holy Grail quest, son of Sir Launcelot and Elaine of Corbin.'

memes.label: Sir Galihud
memes.description: 'A knight of Sir Launcelot kindred; fought in tournaments and was among the ten knights who departed on the Grail Quest from Arthur court.'

memes.label: Sir Gareth
memes.description: 'Arthur''s nephew and Gawaine''s youngest brother in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — the "Fair Unknown" who proves worth through service rather than name, and whose accidental death at Launcelot''s hands triggers the final tragedy.'

memes.label: Sir Gawaine
memes.description: 'Arthur''s nephew and one of the foremost knights of the Round Table in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," whose loyalty to kin drives both his greatest deeds and his most destructive vendetta against Sir Launcelot.'

memes.label: Sir Gilbert the Bastard
memes.description: 'A knight called one of the best knights of the world, slain in battle; his body was kept in a chapel by the sorceress Hellawes.'

memes.label: Sir Gilmere
memes.description: 'Sir Gilmere, a knight who encountered Sir Launcelot in the forest and witnessed the aftermath of a fierce combat.'

memes.label: Sir Gingalin
memes.description: 'Sir Gingalin, son of Sir Gawaine, who encountered Sir Tristram at the Castle of Tintagil'

memes.label: Sir Griflet
memes.description: 'Son of Cardol, one of the first knights made by King Arthur, who proved himself in the early wars and earned Merlin''s prophecy that he would be "a passing good man."'

memes.label: Sir Gringamore
memes.description: 'Brother of Dame Lionesse and ally of Sir Gareth in the Castle Perilous episode of Le Morte d''Arthur'

memes.label: Sir Hebes le Renoumes
memes.description: 'Squire to Sir Tristram who was knighted by him; known as Hebes le Renoumes, he guarded Tristram secret identity in Ireland and saved his life.'

memes.label: Sir Ironside
memes.description: 'A knight known as the Red Knight of the Red Launds, who fought against Sir Gareth and later rode in fellowship with Sir Tristram and Sir Lamorak'
aliases: The Red Knight of the Red Launds

memes.label: Sir Kay
memes.description: 'Arthur''s foster-brother and seneschal of the Round Table in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — a loyal administrator whose practical competence and sharp tongue serve as foil to the more romantic knights of the court.'

memes.label: Sir Kehydius
memes.description: 'Son of King Howel of Brittany, brother of Isoud la Blanche Mains, who died tragically for the love of La Beale Isoud.'

memes.label: Sir La Cote Male Taile
memes.description: 'A young knight mocked by Sir Kay as "La Cote Male Taile" (the Ill-Fitting Coat) who proved his worth through determined questing and won a place at the Round Table'
aliases: The Knight of the Ill-Fitting Coat

memes.label: Sir Lambegus
memes.description: 'A knight in the service of Sir Tristram, who fought against Sir Palamides and was wounded in defence of his lord'

memes.label: Sir Lamorak
memes.description: 'Sir Lamorak de Galis, one of the great knights of the Round Table, brother of Sir Percivale, renowned for his marvellous deeds of arms in tournament and battle.'

memes.label: Sir Launcelot
memes.description: 'The greatest knight of the Round Table, whose peerless prowess is matched only by the tragedy of his love for Queen Guenever — a love that inspires his greatness, prevents his spiritual fulfilment in the Sangreal quest, and ultimately destroys Arthur''s kingdom.'

memes.label: Sir Lionel
memes.description: 'Son of King Bors of Gaul, brother of Sir Bors de Ganis, and cousin of Sir Launcelot; a Knight of the Round Table who participated in the Roman War and the Quest of the Holy Grail'

memes.label: Sir Lucan
memes.description: 'Knight of the Round Table and butler of King Arthur''s court, renowned for his encounter with Sir Tristram'

memes.label: Sir Lucas
memes.description: 'Butler of King Arthur''s court, formerly a duke, who served in the royal household and fought in the battle against the eleven kings'

memes.label: Sir Mador de la Porte
memes.description: 'Sir Mador de la Porte, a knight of the Round Table who fought Sir Launcelot in a tournament and suffered a memorable fall'

memes.label: Sir Marhaus
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table, the son of the King of Ireland, known for his great size and chivalrous conduct in battle'

memes.label: Sir Meliagaunce
memes.description: 'A knight who fought against Sir Lamorak over a matter of love and honour, and later encountered Sir Bleoberis'

memes.label: Sir Meliot
memes.description: 'Knight of the Round Table, Sir Meliot of Logres was mortally wounded and healed by the sword of Sir Launcelot in the Chapel Perilous'

memes.label: Sir Morganor
memes.description: 'Sir Morganor (also called Morganore or King Morganore), a seneschal and knight who served in the early wars of King Arthur and later faced Sir Tristram in tournament.'

memes.label: Sir Nabon
memes.description: 'The giant lord of the Isle of Servage who hated King Arthur''s knights and was slain by Sir Tristram.'

memes.label: Sir Nerovens de Lile
memes.description: 'Sir Nerovens de Lile, a knight made by Sir Launcelot du Lake, who later fought against his own lord and was reconciled through the grace of Launcelot'

memes.label: Sir Ontzlake
memes.description: 'The younger brother of Sir Damas, a good knight of prowess who was made a knight of King Arthur''s Round Table.'

memes.label: Sir Palamides
memes.description: 'A noble Saracen knight of the Round Table, rival of Sir Tristram for the love of La Beale Isoud'

memes.label: Sir Palomides
memes.description: 'A Saracen knight of the Round Table in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," whose pursuit of the Questing Beast and unrequited love for La Beale Isoud mark him as one of the most complex figures in the cycle.'

memes.label: Sir Pelleas
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table whose love for the lady Ettard ended in betrayal by Sir Gawaine and rescue by the Lady of the Lake'

memes.label: Sir Pellinore
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table and king who pursued the Questing Beast; father of Sir Percivale and Sir Tor, slain by Gawaine in revenge for the death of King Lot.'

memes.label: Sir Percivale
memes.description: 'One of the three Grail knights in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," whose simplicity and spiritual purity allow him to achieve what more worldly knights cannot — a figure of innocent faith as the path to divine vision.'

memes.label: Sir Perimones
memes.description: 'Sir Perimones, the Red Knight, brother of Sir Persant of Inde, overcome by Sir Gareth and later his loyal servant'

memes.label: Sir Persant
memes.description: 'Sir Persant of Inde, the Knight of Indigo, was one of the four colour-coded brother knights overcome by Sir Beaumains on his quest to rescue Dame Lioness'

memes.label: Sir Persides de Bloise
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table and companion of Sir Tristram, known for his loyalty and his role in revealing Sir Palomides''s identity to King Arthur''s court'

memes.label: Sir Petipase
memes.description: 'Sir Petipase of Winchelsea, a knight who appears in the tournament narratives of the later books of Le Morte dArthur'

memes.label: Sir Phelot
memes.description: 'Sir Phelot, a knight of the forest who set an ambush for Sir Launcelot and fought him in a woodland encounter'

memes.label: Sir Plenorius
memes.description: 'A noble knight defeated by Sir Launcelot, who yielded his tower and livelihood before being recommended to the Round Table'

memes.label: Sir Priamus
memes.description: 'A Saracen knight who fought Sir Gawaine, was christened, and became a duke and knight of the Round Table under King Arthur'

memes.label: Sir Raynold
memes.description: 'A knight who fought alongside his brothers in a woodland encounter, remembered for his strength and the bond of kinship that defined his brief appearance'

memes.label: Sir Sadok
memes.description: 'Sir Sadok, a knight associated with Sir Tristram who fought at the Tournament of the Castle of Maidens'

memes.label: Sir Safere
memes.description: 'A Saracen knight, brother of Sir Palamides and Sir Segwarides; converted to Christianity and knighted at the Round Table.'

memes.label: Sir Sagramore le Desirous
memes.description: 'A knight of the Round Table known as Sagramore le Desirous, a steadfast companion who appears in tournaments and adventures throughout the chronicle'

memes.label: Sir Segwarides
memes.description: 'A Saracen knight of Cornwall, brother of Sir Palamides and Sir Safere; his wife became embroiled in the Tristram cycle, leading to a bitter conflict with Sir Tristram.'

memes.label: Sir Suppinabiles
memes.description: 'Sir Suppinabiles, a knight of Brittany who brought news of Sir Tristram to King Arthur''s court'

memes.label: Sir Tor
memes.description: 'Son of King Pellinore, a knight of the Round Table whose quiet prowess earned King Arthur''s judgment: "he saith little and he doth much more."'
aliases: Sir Tor le Fise de Vayshoure

memes.label: Sir Tristram
memes.description: 'The second-greatest knight of the Round Table after Sir Launcelot, whose love for La Beale Isoud — his uncle King Mark''s wife — defines his greatness and seals his doom.'

memes.label: Sir Turquine
memes.description: 'A villainous knight of extraordinary strength, keeper of a prison of defeated knights, slain by Sir Launcelot in single combat'

memes.label: Sir Ulfius
memes.description: 'A noble knight who served both King Uther Pendragon and King Arthur, acting as chamberlain and trusted counsellor.'

memes.label: Sir Uwaine
memes.description: 'Son of King Uriens of Gore and Morgan le Fay, a knight of the Round Table known for saving his father from his mother''s treachery.'

memes.label: Sovereign
memes.description: 'Sovereign lordship and the nature of supreme political authority in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur, from Arthur''s imperial claims to the personal sovereignty of the lady in courtly love.'

memes.label: Spear
memes.description: 'The spear as the primary weapon of the mounted charge, tournament implement, and symbolic object in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Sword
memes.description: 'The sword as weapon of war, symbol of sovereignty, and narrative token in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Sword in the Stone
memes.description: 'The miraculous event in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" by which Arthur is revealed as the true king — a sword set in a stone that only the rightful sovereign could draw, combining divine election with chivalric trial.'

memes.label: Test
memes.description: 'Tests and trials in Malory function as narrative mechanisms that reveal knightly identity, prove virtue, and distinguish the worthy from the unworthy in "Le Morte d''Arthur."'

memes.label: The Church
memes.description: 'The ecclesiastical institution that crowns kings, sanctifies marriages, buries the dead, and provides the moral and ritual framework within which the chivalric world of Arthur operates.'

memes.label: Tournament
memes.description: 'Organized contests of martial skill in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur," serving as both displays of chivalric prowess and narrative devices for advancing plot and character.'

memes.label: Treason
memes.description: 'The gravest moral and legal violation in Malory''s "Le Morte d''Arthur" — the betrayal of sworn loyalty to king, kin, or fellowship, punishable by death and destined to destroy the Round Table from within.'

memes.label: Uther Pendragon
memes.description: 'King of all England and father of King Arthur, whose death sets the stage for Arthur''s rise and the drawing of the sword from the stone.'

memes.label: Virtue
memes.description: 'Virtue as moral excellence and its role in chivalric identity, magical objects, and the spiritual economy of Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Worship
memes.description: 'Worship in Malory denotes knightly honor, reputation, and the public worth earned through deeds of arms and noble conduct; the central currency of chivalric identity in "Le Morte d''Arthur."'

memes.label: Wound
memes.description: 'Wounds and injury in chivalric combat, their narrative function, and the quest for healing in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'

memes.label: Wrath
memes.description: 'Knightly wrath — its causes, manifestations, and destructive consequences — as a recurring narrative driver in Malory''s Le Morte d''Arthur.'
